title: VaultConfigurator.create Has No Access Control
reason: Configurator being permissionless is an expected factory pattern; this does not grant control over existing vaults or bypass role checks.

title: Fee Calculation Can Overflow
reason: Fee math uses checked arithmetic plus `Math.mulDiv`; claimed overflow is not a practical protocol bug under Solidity 0.8 checks.

title: VaultModule Missing Subvault Validation in Pull/Push Assets
reason: `_pullAssets/_pushAssets` call `riskManager.modifySubvaultBalance`, which enforces connected subvault validation via `requireValidSubvault`.

title: SignatureQueue Price Validation Allows Price Manipulation
reason: Order price is consensus-signed and then validated against oracle bounds; this is intended signed-order behavior, not an unconditional manipulation primitive.

title: DepositQueue Deposit Can Be Front-Run
reason: Generic mempool visibility/MEV observation without a concrete protocol logic flaw.

title: FeeRecipient Can Be Changed to Any Address
reason: Owner-controlled fee recipient is an expected governance trust assumption, not a vulnerability by itself.

title: Consensus Allows Signature Replay Across Different Orders
reason: Signatures are bound to typed order hash; queue nonce checks prevent replay after execution.

title: RiskManager convertToShares Can Revert on Invalid Price
reason: Reverting on suspicious/zero reports is intentional safety behavior, not a bug.

title: BasicRedeemHook Potential Reentrancy in Loop
reason: Relevant external entrypoints are guarded with reentrancy protections; no concrete exploit path was substantiated.

title: LidoDepositHook Unchecked Balance Change
reason: Hook uses reverting external calls (`safeIncreaseAllowance`, `wrap`, `withdraw`); balance-delta accounting is intentional.

title: ShareModule getLiquidAssets Uses msg.sender Without Verification
reason: Function verifies caller is a registered redeem queue for the asset; no missing authorization found.

title: Factory create Uses Predictable Salt
reason: Deterministic CREATE2 salt here does not yield a practical exploit; frontrunning cannot steal ownership/initialization semantics.

title: TokenizedShareManager Auto-Claims Shares Without Access Control
reason: Not a standalone access-control issue; merged under gas/liveness finding for unbounded auto-claim costs.

title: EigenLayerVerifier Incomplete delegateTo Verification
reason: Verifier validates call shape/roles; signature expiry semantics are enforced by downstream EigenLayer contracts.

title: RiskManager Limit Check After State Modification
reason: Checks are performed before storage writes in the same transaction context; claimed race window is incorrect.

title: ShareManager claimShares Can Be Called By Anyone
reason: Public claim-on-behalf is a benign helper pattern here; no concrete harmful state violation shown.

title: BitmaskVerifier Bitmask Length Validation Bug
reason: `bitmask.length == data.length + 0x60` matches documented format (96-byte fixed segment plus calldata mask).

title: RedirectingDepositHook May Leave Assets Undeposited
reason: Partial allocation when limits are reached is expected behavior, not a correctness/security fault.

title: FeeManager calculateFee Has No Access Control
reason: Public view fee calculation is not a security issue.

title: ShareModule createQueue Does Not Verify Asset Supported by Oracle
reason: `createQueue` explicitly checks `IOracle($.oracle).isSupportedAsset(asset)` and oracle is initialized non-zero.

title: Verifier setMerkleRoot Has Insufficient Access Control
reason: Role-based root updates are intentional; this is governance/key-management risk, not an implementation flaw.
